headline: Pakistan Removes 18% Tax on Menstrual Products After Campaign
meta_description: A Pakistani activist successfully led a campaign that convinced the government to eliminate the 18% tax on menstrual pads, making them more affordable.
summary_short: Following a grassroots campaign, Pakistan has scrapped the 18% tax on menstrual products, making them significantly more affordable for women.
body:
A Pakistani activist who grew up in a household where menstrual pads were rationed among six women has helped lead a campaign with a concrete victory: the government has removed the 18% tax on period products, substantially lowering their cost.

This change addresses a real barrier to menstrual health and dignity. Taxing essential hygiene products as luxury goods created hardship for many families, forcing difficult choices between affordability and health. By removing this tax, Pakistan has taken a step toward ensuring menstrual products remain accessible to all who need them.

The campaign represents the power of lived experience combined with sustained advocacy. The activist's personal understanding of how tax policy affects everyday families gave weight to the push for change. This victory demonstrates how persistent grassroots efforts can reshape government policy on matters that affect public health and dignity.
